evaluation.summary: A variance-based adaptive weighting scheme applied to Wasserstein distances for prime residue classes modulo 210 produced only a marginal 1.35% reduction in temporal variance, failing to meet the predefined significance threshold.
next_directions: Scale the adaptive weighting to larger modulus values and assess whether variance reductions become statistically significant.
Conduct a rigorous hypothesis test using bootstrapped confidence intervals to determine if observed changes are beyond experimental noise.

Compare the Wasserstein proxy against alternative distance metrics (e.g., KL divergence, Earth Mover's Distance) to evaluate the robustness of the adaptive scheme across different statistical measures.
